display.purpose: Brew-mode timer only. Single largest type element on the screen, weight 700, tabular-nums.

headline.purpose: Setup-mode dose number. Same rhythm as the timer, smaller scale.

title.purpose: Brew-mode recipe name in the header. Amount values in the amounts table.

body.purpose: Step labels, recipe notes (italicized), amount keys, default text.

label.purpose: Section heads above each control group. 11px, 0.08em letter-spacing, uppercase. Reserved for tiny heads only.

shadows.name: cta-lift
shadows.purpose: Applied only to the bottom-anchored Start and Stop buttons. The single elevation in the system.

motion.name: press-firm
motion.purpose: Default press feedback on pills and stepper buttons. Touch-target acknowledgement.

motion.name: press-soft
motion.purpose: Press feedback on the bottom CTAs. Subtler than press-firm because the lift shadow already reads as object-pressing.

motion.name: press-stepper
motion.purpose: Press feedback on the dose +/- stepper buttons. Slightly firmer because they are pressed more often.

motion.name: transition-color
motion.purpose: Pill state changes. The only color transition in the system.

motion.name: transition-step
motion.purpose: Step row advancing from upcoming to current to done.

breakpoints.name: column
breakpoints.purpose: Single column max-width. Mobile-first; desktop just gets more whitespace.

components.name: Recipe Pill
components.description: Recipe selector. Inactive reads as quiet (Oat Foam Deep on Ash); active is the loudest pill in the row (Roast Brown on White Ceramic). 44px touch target.
components.css: .ds-pill { flex: 1 1 auto; min-height: 44px; padding: 10px 14px; border-radius: 22px; background: #ebe6dc; color: #777; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; border: 1.5px solid transparent; cursor: pointer; text-align: center; transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s; user-select: none; } .ds-pill:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-pill[aria-pressed="true"] { background: #6f4e37; color: #ffffff; border-color: #6f4e37; }

components.name: Dose Stepper Button
components.description: 44x44 stepper, 12px radius, Oat Foam Deep background. Used in pairs (- and +) on either side of the dose number. Disabled at the dose clamp boundaries.
components.css: .ds-dose-btn { width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 12px; border: none; background: #ebe6dc; color: #222; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; } .ds-dose-btn:disabled { opacity: 0.35; cursor: not-allowed; } .ds-dose-btn:active:not(:disabled) { transform: scale(0.95); }

components.name: Amounts Table
components.description: Read-only key/value table on Oat Foam, divided by hairline Parchment Line dividers. Tabular-nums on values. The whole card has no header; the Label component sits above it in the page flow.
components.html:
Water (total)333 g
Bloom40 - 60 g
Pour 1 by 1:15200 g
GrindMedium-fine
Total time~3:00
components.css: .ds-amounts { background: #f3eee2; border-radius: 14px; padding: 6px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; max-width: 480px; } .ds-amount-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; padding: 11px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #e8e2d4; font-size: 14px; } .ds-amount-row:last-child { border-bottom: none; } .ds-amount-key { color: #777; } .ds-amount-val { font-weight: 600; color: #222; }

components.name: Step List
components.description: Brew-mode step list. Three states per row: upcoming (Ink/Ash), done (Fog dim), current (Roast Brown background, White Ceramic text). The current row is the second-loudest element on the screen after the timer.
components.html:
0:00Bloom 40-60 g, swirl
1:15Pour 1: Pour to 200 g
1:45Pour 2: Pour to 333 g
3:00Drawdown: Stir, swirl, let it draw
components.css: .ds-steps { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: #f3eee2; border-radius: 14px; padding: 10px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #222; max-width: 480px; } .ds-step { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 56px 1fr; gap: 10px; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 14px; transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-step-time { color: #777; } .ds-step-done { color: #aaa; } .ds-step-done .ds-step-time { color: #aaa; } .ds-step-current { background: #6f4e37; color: #ffffff; } .ds-step-current .ds-step-time { color: #ffffff; opacity: 0.85; }

components.name: Big Timer
components.description: Brew-mode timer block. The Display type element (700, 64px, tabular-nums, Roast Brown) sits directly on the page surface with no card. The target line below uses body type with the active step's target wrapped in <strong>.
components.css: .ds-timer { text-align: center; padding: 8px 0 4px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 480px; } .ds-timer-big { font-size: 64px; font-weight: 700; color: #6f4e37; line-height: 1; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-timer-target { font-size: 14px; color: #777; margin-top: 6px; min-height: 1em; } .ds-timer-target strong { color: #222; font-weight: 600; }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Bottom-anchored Start brew CTA. Roast Brown background, White Ceramic text, the only element in the system that carries the cta-lift shadow. Full-width within the 480px column.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { border: none; padding: 16px; border-radius: 14px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; width: 100%; max-width: 480px; background: #6f4e37; color: #ffffff; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.99); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Bottom-anchored Stop CTA. Same shape and position as the Primary, but Burnt Brick background. The color difference is the entire signal.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { border: none; padding: 16px; border-radius: 14px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; width: 100%; max-width: 480px; background: #a85a3c; color: #ffffff; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: scale(0.99); }

narrative.overview:
The interface should feel like a hand-shaped object you reach for first thing in the morning: warm, matte, a little weighty, used a hundred times. Not gallery-clean and not lab-precise. The palette is roasted-bean and unbleached paper. The typography is a system stack with tabular numerals so the dose, the timer, and the pour milestones all sit on a steady grid even when the digits change. The shapes are softly rounded, the touch targets are forgiving, and the only motion is a 3% press-down on tap so a wet thumb feels acknowledged.

The system is mobile-first and explicitly anti-density. The whole brewing flow lives in a single 480px column so a phone propped against a kettle shows everything at once and a desktop window just gets more whitespace. Nothing scrolls horizontally, no panel slides in, no modal appears, no tooltip explains. If a glance from across the counter while pouring water can't catch what's happening, the design has failed.

It rejects the SEO-recipe-blog shape (sticky banners, hero photos, ad rails) and the pro-barista lab shape (black canvas, neon green readouts, dense overlays) in equal measure. The kitchen is the context, not a dashboard.
keyCharacteristics: One column, one screen, one knob (the dose).
Cream and roast-brown light, cocoa and crema dark - the same warmth either way.
Tabular numerals everywhere a number lives.
Tactile feedback (scale(0.97) on :active) instead of decorative animation.
Audio is the primary timing cue; the visible timer is reassurance.

rules.name: The One-Brown Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one accent. In light mode it is Roast Brown; in dark mode it is Crema. The Start button, the active pill, the current step, the dose number, the timer - they all wear the same color so the eye can find 'what matters now' without thinking. Adding a second accent is a system-level change, not a screen-level decision.

rules.name: The No-Bleached-White Rule
rules.body: Never use #ffffff as a surface. White Ceramic is reserved for foreground text on Roast Brown and Burnt Brick, where its purity reads as intentional. On any background larger than a button, use Paper Cream.

rules.name: The Theme-Mirror Rule
rules.body: Light and dark mode are the same design with reversed tonal direction - same warmth, same hierarchy, same component shapes. Tokens are paired (Roast Brown vs Crema, Paper Cream vs Dark Roast, Oat Foam vs Cocoa Husk). Don't ship a feature with one mode polished and the other dim.

rules.name: The Tabular-Numerals Rule
rules.body: font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums is set on the body globally, not per element. Every digit - the dose, the amounts, the timer, the step times, the elapsed time in the header - sits on the same fixed grid. A timer that jitters when seconds tick is unacceptable.

rules.name: The Italic-For-Notes Rule
rules.body: Italic is used exactly once: the recipe-notes line under the amounts table. Italic = 'context, not instruction.'

rules.name: The No-Caps-Headlines Rule
rules.body: The 0.08em letter-spaced uppercase treatment is reserved for tiny section labels (11px). Never apply it to anything bigger than that - a 16px uppercase headline is the SaaS-marketing fingerprint this system rejects.

rules.name: The Tonal-Layering Rule
rules.body: Depth is conveyed by the four-step warm-neutral ramp, not by shadows. To raise a surface, step it inward by one tone (page to card to control). Don't reach for box-shadow.

rules.name: The One-Lift Rule
rules.body: Only the bottom-anchored primary CTA carries the cta-lift shadow. If a future component wants elevation, the question to answer first is 'should this become the new bottom CTA?' - almost always the answer is no.

rules.name: The Tactile-And-Forgiving Rule
rules.body: Every interactive element is at least 44px tall, has generous padding, and presses down 1-3% on :active. Damp fingers, sleepy thumbs. If a target is below 44px or has no press feedback, it has not earned its place in this interface.

rules.name: The No-Hover-States Rule
rules.body: The interface targets touch as the primary input. Hover treatments are not used - color/state changes happen on :active (mouse-down, finger-down) so a glance at the desktop version reads identically to the phone.

rules.name: The Single-Knob Doctrine
rules.body: The dose is the only input on the calculator. Adding bidirectional inputs (edit-water, edit-ratio) is an explicit non-feature. If a number changes on the screen, it is downstream of the dose.

dos: Do keep the entire brewing flow in a single 480px column. The setup view and the brew view share one screen; the transition is content-only, not a route.
Do use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on every numeric element. The dose, the amounts, the timer, the step times - all share one digit grid.
Do ship light and dark mode together. Dark mode is mirrored from light, not bolted on.
Do rely on tonal layering (page to card to control to border) for depth. Each step is roughly 5% darker than the previous one.
Do anchor the primary CTA at the bottom of the column with margin-top: auto. The Start/Stop button is the single committed action on the screen and should always sit under the thumb.
Do use a transform: scale(0.97) press feedback on touch targets. It costs nothing, it survives reduced-motion, and it answers the wet finger.
Do treat the audio chime as the primary cue. The visible timer is reassurance; the ear leads, the eye follows.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color. Roast Brown is the only 'this matters' color in light mode; Crema is the only one in dark.
Don't use #ffffff or #000000 anywhere as a surface. White Ceramic is foreground-only; Dark Roast (#2a2620) is as black as this system gets.
Don't add gradients, gradient text, or background-clip: text. The palette is matte. Glossy is the wrong shape.
Don't apply glassmorphism, backdrop-filter blurs, or translucent layers. The surfaces are tonal-layered ceramic, not stacked frosted glass.
Don't add side-stripe borders (border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent on a card or list item). If a row needs emphasis, swap its background to Roast Brown.
Don't style this like a recipe blog. No hero photos, no sticky CTAs, no 'made it!' buttons, no scrolling content rail, no monetization furniture.
Don't style this like pro-barista hardware. No black canvas, no neon green readouts, no hexagonal data overlays, no 'engineering for caffeine' aesthetic.
Don't add streak counters, progress rings, achievement toasts, or any gamification surface.
Don't add hover treatments. Touch is the primary input.
Don't introduce a modal, drawer, or tooltip. If something needs explaining, it doesn't belong on the screen.
Don't convert any at:0 rinse/preheat into a timer step. The timer's zero is the moment hot water hits coffee.
Don't use uppercase letter-spacing on anything bigger than 11px.
Don't use em dashes anywhere in the UI copy or in this file.
